name: AN INTERVENTIONAL SAFETY AND EFFICACY PHASE 1B/2, OPEN-LABEL STUDY TO INVESTIGATE TOLERABILITY, PK, AND ANTITUMOR ACTIVITY OF VEPDEGESTRANT (ARV-471/PF-07850327), AN ORAL PROTEOLYSIS TARGETING CHIMERA, IN COMBINATION WITH PF-07220060 IN PARTICIPANTS AGED 18 YEARS AND OLDER WITH ER+/HER2- ADVANCED OR METASTATIC BREAST CANCER

titles.text: AN INTERVENTIONAL SAFETY AND EFFICACY PHASE 1B/2, OPEN-LABEL STUDY TO INVESTIGATE TOLERABILITY, PK, AND ANTITUMOR ACTIVITY OF VEPDEGESTRANT (ARV-471/PF-07850327), AN ORAL PROTEOLYSIS TARGETING CHIMERA, IN COMBINATION WITH PF-07220060 IN PARTICIPANTS AGED 18 YEARS AND OLDER WITH ER+/HER2- ADVANCED OR METASTATIC BREAST CANCER
A Study to Investigate the Safety and Antitumor Activity of Vepdegestrant (ARV-471) in Combination With PF-07220060 in Adults With ER+/HER2- Advanced or Metastatic Breast Cancer

amendments.summary: The main purpose of the amendment is to indicate that participants benefitting from treatment (defined as complete/partial response or stable disease as per RECIST v1.1) will continue to have access to the study intervention as part of the study. This amendment also introduces a reduced Schedule of Activities (SoA) allowing most clinical assessments per standard of care or clinical judgment of the treating physician. Reduced data recording will maintain safety and tumor assessment (performed as per standard of care).

primaryReason.otherReason: The main purpose of the amendment is to indicate that participants benefitting from treatment (defined as complete/partial response or stable disease as per RECIST v1.1) will continue to have access to the study intervention as part of the study. This amendment also introduces a reduced Schedule of Activities (SoA) allowing most clinical assessments per standard of care or clinical judgment of the treating physician. Reduced data recording will maintain safety and tumor assessment (performed as per standard of care).

amendments.name: Amendment 2

changes.name: 2 — Section 1.3. Table 2 and Table 4 Schedule of Activities
changes.summary: Adding a requirement to perform a pre-dose ECG at Cycle 3.
changes.rationale: To address health authority feedback to allow for increased ECG monitoring due to potential risk of QTc prolongation of vepdegestrant

changedSections.sectionTitle: Section 1.3. Table 2 and Table 4 Schedule of Activities

changes.name: 2 — Section 4.1. Overall Design
changes.summary: Adding intraparticipant dose escalation criteria for Phase 1b participants.
changes.rationale: To address health authority feedback to clarify intra- patient dose escalation criteria

changedSections.sectionTitle: Section 4.1. Overall Design

changes.name: 2 — Section 4.3.1.3. Non- Hematological DLTs
changes.summary: Adding the radiological imaging to rule out pancreatitis for participants who experience a Grade ≥3 amylase or lipase.
changes.rationale: To address health authority feedback to modify the definition of the non- hematological DLTs in case of Grade 3 amylase or lipase to exclude pancreatitis.

changedSections.sectionTitle: Section 4.3.1.3. Non- Hematological DLTs

changes.name: 2 — Section 1.1 Synopsis and Section 5.2. Exclusion Criteria, Medical Conditions: Criterion # 5
changes.summary: Modified eligibility criteria to provide sex specific thresholds for QTcF and to exclude participants with risk factors for QT prolongation.
changes.rationale: To address health authority feedback to exclude patients who present with corrected QT-Fridericia method (QTcF) > 470 ms (for women) and > 450 ms (for men) on screening ECG and with risk factors for QT prolongation (eg, electrolyte abnormalities) unless mitigated or corrected prior to enrollment

changedSections.sectionTitle: Section 1.1 Synopsis and Section 5.2. Exclusion Criteria, Medical Conditions: Criterion # 5

changes.summary: Included additional country specific exclusion criteria for participants in France
changes.rationale: To address health authority feedback

The contact information for the study medical monitor is documented in the Study Team Contact List and the Emergency Contact Card is being replaced by a study information card.
changes.rationale: The process for contacting a medically qualified individual has changed from a medical escalation process via a Pfizer Call Center to direct clinical team contact using a Study Team Contact List. The Emergency Contact Card is being replaced by a study information card and will no longer be referenced.

versions.rationale: To investigate tolerability, pharmacokinetics (PK), and antitumor activity of vepdegestrant (ARV-471) in combination with PF-07220060 in adults with ER+/HER2- advanced or metastatic breast cancer.

population.name: Main Population
population.label: Participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC, who have been previously treated with up to 2 lines of prior therapies for advanced/metastatic disease. Phase 2 participants must have at least 1 measurable lesion as per RECIST v1.1 and must have received 1 line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen.

notes.text: includesHealthySubjects inferred as false because the protocol population is disease-specific (ER+/HER2- advanced/metastatic breast cancer).

cohorts.name: Dose Level 1
cohorts.label: Dose Level 1
cohorts.description: Dose Level 1 | Vepdegestrant: 200 mg QD | PF-07220060: 100 mg BID

cohorts.name: Dose Level 2
cohorts.label: Dose Level 2
cohorts.description: Dose Level 2 | Vepdegestrant: 200 mg QD | PF-07220060: 300 mg BID

population.description: Participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC, who have been previously treated with up to 2 lines of prior therapies for advanced/metastatic disease. Phase 2 participants must have at least 1 measurable lesion as per RECIST v1.1 and must have received 1 line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen.

arms.name: vepdegestrant and PF-07220060
arms.label: vepdegestrant and PF-07220060
arms.description: Vepdegestrant and PF-07220060. The starting dose level of vepdegestrant will be 200 mg QD and the starting dose level of PF-07220060 will be 100 mg BID. Vepdegestrant and PF-07220060 must be taken with food. Vepdegestrant and the first of two daily doses of PF-07220060 should preferably be taken in the morning at approximately the same time.

studyDesigns.name: Open-Label Phase 1b/2 Study Design
studyDesigns.label: C4891026 is a prospective, open-label, multicenter, Phase 1b/2 study to evaluate the safety, antitumor activity, and PK of vepdegestrant in combination with PF-07220060 in the treatment of participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC.

epochs.name: Screening
epochs.label: Screening
epochs.description: The screening period will occur within 28 days prior to first dose.

epochs.name: Combination Treatment Period 1
epochs.label: Combination Treatment Period 1 Cycle = 28 days
epochs.description: Study intervention will be administered in 28-day cycles.

epochs.name: Post-Treatment Follow-Up
epochs.label: Post-Treatment Follow-Up
epochs.description: At least 28 calendar days (+7 days) after discontinuation of study intervention or prior if a new anticancer therapy is initiated (whichever comes first), participants will return to site to undergo the safety follow-up assessments as reported in the SoA.

epochs.name: Survival Follow-Up
epochs.label: Survival Follow-Up
epochs.description: For Phase 2 as reported in the SoA, survival status will be collected in the CRF every 3 months (±7 days) from the last dose of study treatment, including subsequent anticancer therapies (eg, start and stop dates, BOR and date of PD of the subsequent therapy), until death, withdrawal of consent, lost to follow-up, or end of study, whichever occurs first.

elements.name: EL1
elements.label: Informed Consent Process
elements.description: Informed consent must be obtained within 28 days of enrollment and prior to any protocol-required assessments being performed (Section 10.1.3). Procedures conducted as part of the participant's routine clinical practice and obtained before signing of the ICD may be utilized for screening as specified in Section 8.1.

transitionEndRule.name: EndRule1
transitionEndRule.text: Informed consent must be obtained within 28 days of enrollment and prior to any protocol-required assessments being performed (Section 10.1.3).

transitionStartRule.name: StartRule1
transitionStartRule.text: Informed consent must be obtained within 28 days of enrollment and prior to any protocol-required assessments being performed (Section 10.1.3).

elements.name: EL2
elements.label: Baseline Documentation
elements.description: Baseline Documentation includes Demography and Medical/Oncological History, Physical Examination/Vital Signs, Height (at Screening Only) and Body Weight, ECOG Performance Status and other baseline assessments as specified in the SoA.

elements.name: EL3
elements.label: Allocation to Study Intervention
elements.description: Allocation to study intervention must be completed after eligibility has been confirmed. Study intervention administration should start ≤3 days after assignment.

transitionEndRule.name: EndRule3
transitionEndRule.text: Study intervention administration should start ≤3 days after assignment.

transitionStartRule.name: StartRule3
transitionStartRule.text: Allocation to study intervention must be completed after eligibility has been confirmed.

elements.name: EL4
elements.label: Vepdgestrant and PF-07220060
elements.description: The starting dose level of vepdegestrant will be 200 mg QD and the starting dose level of PF-07220060 will be 100 mg BID. Vepdegestrant and PF-07220060 must be taken with food. Vepdegestrant and the first of two daily doses of PF-07220060 should preferably be taken in the morning at approximately the same time.

elements.name: EL5
elements.label: Blood Samples for vepdegestrant and ARV-473 PK
elements.description: Predose samples to be collected within 2 hours prior to dosing; postdose samples 4-8 hours after dosing. See Section 8.5 and Table 2 for specific timepoints.

transitionStartRule.name: StartRule5
transitionStartRule.text: Predose samples to be collected within 2 hours prior to dosing

elements.name: EL6
elements.label: Post-Treatment Follow-Up
elements.description: At least 28 calendar days (+7 days) after discontinuation of study intervention or prior if a new anticancer therapy is initiated (whichever comes first), participants will return to site to undergo the safety follow-up assessments as reported in the SoA.

transitionEndRule.name: EndRule6
transitionEndRule.text: Participants continuing to experience AEs at this point following discontinuation of treatment will continue to be followed at least every 4 weeks until resolution or determination, in the clinical judgment of the investigator, that no further improvement is expected.

transitionStartRule.name: StartRule6
transitionStartRule.text: At least 28 calendar days (+7 days) after discontinuation of study intervention or prior if a new anticancer therapy is initiated (whichever comes first)

elements.name: EL7
elements.label: Survival Follow-Up
elements.description: For Phase 2 as reported in the SoA, survival status will be collected in the CRF every 3 months (±7 days) from the last dose of study treatment, including subsequent anticancer therapies (eg, start and stop dates, BOR and date of PD of the subsequent therapy), until death, withdrawal of consent, lost to follow-up, or end of study, whichever occurs first. Telephone contact is acceptable.

transitionEndRule.name: EndRule7
transitionEndRule.text: until death, withdrawal of consent, lost to follow-up, or end of study, whichever occurs first.

transitionStartRule.name: StartRule7
transitionStartRule.text: every 3 months (±7 days) from the last dose of study treatment

estimands.name: Phase1b Primary Estimand
estimands.description:
DLT rate estimated based on data from DLT-evaluable participants during the DLT observation period (Cycle 1). The estimand has the following attributes:

 Population: DLT-evaluable participants with A/MBC, defined by the inclusion and exclusion criteria to reflect the targeted population of the treatment, who receive at least 1 dose of the study interventions in Phase 1b and either experience DLT(s) during the DLT observation period or complete the DLT observation period without DLT. Participants without DLTs who receive less than 75% of the planned dose of each study drug during DLT observation period for reasons other than treatment-related toxicity are not evaluable for DLT. All participants deemed non-evaluable for DLT may be replaced. See Section 4.3.1 for the DLT definition.

 Endpoint: Occurrence of DLTs during the DLT observation period.

 Population-level summary measure: DLT rate defined as the number of DLT evaluable participants with DLTs in the DLT observation period divided by the number of DLT evaluable participants in the DLT observation period.
estimands.populationSummary: DLT-evaluable participants in Phase 1b who received ≥1 dose and either experienced DLTs during Cycle 1 or completed the DLT observation period; participants who received <75% of planned dose for non-treatment-related reasons are non-evaluable.

intercurrentEvents.name: Non-evaluable for DLT due to <75% dosing (non-treatment-related)
intercurrentEvents.text: Participants without DLTs who receive <75% of planned dose during DLT observation period for reasons other than treatment-related toxicity are not evaluable for DLT.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: Exclude from DLT-evaluable population (per protocol definition)
intercurrentEvents.description: Participants without DLTs who receive less than 75% of the planned dose of each study drug during DLT observation period for reasons other than treatment-related toxicity are not evaluable for DLT.

estimands.name: Phase2 Primary Estimand
estimands.description:
The treatment effect of vepdegestrant in combination with PF-07220060 on ORR per RECIST v1.1. response criteria as determined by investigator assessment. The estimand has the following attributes:

 Population: participants with A/MBC, as defined by the inclusion and exclusion criteria to reflect the targeted population of the treatment, who received at least 1 dose of study intervention.

 Endpoint: OR defined as confirmed CR or PR per RECIST v1.1. response criteria as determined by investigator, from the date of first dose until the first documentation of PD, death, or start of new anticancer therapy. Both CR and PR must be confirmed by repeat assessments performed no fewer than 28 days after the criteria for response are first met.

 Intercurrent events: All data collected after an intercurrent event of subsequent anticancer therapy will be excluded. All response assessments regardless of gaps in disease assessments will be considered. Participants who do not have a post-baseline disease assessment due to early PD, who receive anticancer therapies other than the study intervention prior to achieving an objective response, or who die, experience documented PD, or stop disease assessments for any reason prior to achieving an objective response will be counted as non-responders.

 Population-level summary measure: ORR defined as the proportion of participants in the analysis population with an OR and 2-sided 95% CI for ORR.
estimands.populationSummary: Participants with advanced/metastatic breast cancer who received ≥1 dose of study intervention (analysis population for Phase 2).

intercurrentEvents.name: Subsequent anticancer therapy
intercurrentEvents.text: Subsequent anticancer therapy: data collected after start of new anticancer therapy will be excluded from response assessment.
intercurrentEvents.description: All data collected after an intercurrent event of subsequent anticancer therapy will be excluded.

intercurrentEvents.name: Missing post-baseline assessment due to early PD or death
intercurrentEvents.text: Missing post-baseline assessment due to early PD, death, start of other anticancer therapy, or stopping disease assessments: counted as non-responders.
intercurrentEvents.description: Participants who do not have a post-baseline disease assessment due to early PD, who receive anticancer therapies other than the study intervention prior to achieving an objective response, or who die, experience documented PD, or stop disease assessments for any reason prior to achieving an objective response will be counted as non-responders.

studyDesigns.rationale: C4891026 is a prospective, open-label, multicenter, Phase 1b/2 study to evaluate the safety, antitumor activity, and PK of vepdegestrant in combination with PF-07220060 in the treatment of participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC.

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_01_Ph1b_SafetyTolerability
objectives.text: Assess the safety and tolerability of vepdegestrant combined with PF-07220060 in participants with ER+/HER2- advanced/metastatic breast cancer to select up to two recommended doses for expansion.

endpoints.name: EP_PR_01_DLT
endpoints.text: Occurrence of dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs) during the Cycle 1 DLT observation period in Phase 1b.

objectives.description: To assess safety and tolerability of vepdegestrant in combination with PF- 07220060 in participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC to select up to 2 RDEs for the combination.

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_02_Ph2_AntitumorActivity
objectives.text: Evaluate the clinical antitumor activity of vepdegestrant combined with PF-07220060 in participants with ER+/HER2- advanced/metastatic breast cancer.

endpoints.name: EP_PR_02_ORR
endpoints.text: Confirmed objective response rate (confirmed CR or PR) per investigator assessment using RECIST v1.1.
endpoints.description: Confirmed OR (CR or PR) determined by investigator assessment.

objectives.description: To assess the clinical antitumor activity of vepdegestrant in combination with PF-07220060.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_01_Ph1b_SafetyProfile
objectives.text: Characterize the overall safety profile of vepdegestrant combined with PF-07220060.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_01_AEs
endpoints.text: Adverse events summarized by type, frequency, severity (NCI CTCAE v5.0), seriousness, and relationship to study treatment.
endpoints.description: AEs as characterized by type, frequency, intensity (as graded by NCI CTCAE version 5.0), seriousness, and relationship to vepdegestrant in combination with PF-07220060

endpoints.name: EP_SC_02_LabAbnormalities
endpoints.text: Incidence and characterization of clinically significant laboratory abnormalities.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_03_ECGAbnormalities
endpoints.text: Incidence of ECG abnormalities during study treatment.

objectives.description: To evaluate the overall safety profile.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_02_Ph1b_AntitumorActivity
objectives.text: Evaluate antitumor activity of the combination using investigator assessments of response and time-to-event measures.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_04_ConfirmedOR
endpoints.text: Confirmed objective response (CR or PR) by investigator assessment.
endpoints.description: Confirmed OR (CR or PR) by investigator assessment.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_05_DoR
endpoints.text: Duration of response (DoR) measured from first documentation of confirmed response to objective tumor progression or death.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_06_CBR
endpoints.text: Clinical benefit rate (CBR): confirmed CR or PR at any time, or stable disease ≥ 24 weeks.
endpoints.description: CBR (confirmed CR or PR at any time, or SD ≥24 weeks) by investigator assessment.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_07_PFS
endpoints.text: Progression-free survival (PFS): time from first dose to first documentation of PD or death from any cause.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_08_OS
endpoints.text: Overall survival (OS): time from first dose to death due to any cause.

objectives.description: To evaluate antitumor activity of vepdegestrant in combination with PF-07220060.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_03_PlasmaExposure_PK
objectives.text: Characterize plasma exposure and PK parameters of vepdegestrant, its metabolite ARV-473, and PF-07220060 when administered in combination.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_09_PlasmaConcentrations
endpoints.text: Plasma concentrations of vepdegestrant, ARV-473, and PF-07220060 measured over time.
endpoints.description: Plasma concentrations of vepdegestrant, ARV-473, and PF-07220060.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_10_PKParameters
endpoints.text: Summary PK parameters including steady-state Cmax, Tmax, AUClast and, if data allow, CL/F, Vz/F, and half-life.
endpoints.description: Steady-state Cmax, Tmax, and AUClast of vepdegestrant, ARV-473, and PF-07220060. If data permit CL/F, Vz/F, and t1/2 of vepdegestrant, ARV-473, and PF-07220060.

objectives.description: To evaluate the plasma exposure of vepdegestrant, ARV-473, and PF-07220060 when vepdegestrant and PF-07220060 are given in combination.

objectives.name: OBJ_EX_01_TumorBiology
objectives.text: Explore tumor biology and potential treatment effects using biomarker analyses of tumor tissue and peripheral blood.

endpoints.name: EP_EX_01_BiomarkerMeasurements
endpoints.text: Measure biomarkers (DNA, RNA, protein, or defined cell types) from tumor tissue and peripheral blood.
endpoints.description: Measurements of biomarkers (DNA, RNA, protein, or defined cell types) resulting from analyses of peripheral blood and/or tumor tissue biospecimen.

endpoints.name: EP_EX_02_GenomicsAssociations
endpoints.text: Assess associations between tumor genomic alterations and treatment response or disease biology.
endpoints.description: Associations of tumor gene/genomic alterations with participant treatment responses and disease biology

endpoints.name: EP_EX_03_UGT2B7_Variants
endpoints.text: Characterize allelic variants of UGT2B7 and explore associations with PF-07220060 exposure.

objectives.description: To evaluate tumor biology and explore the treatment effect of vepdegestrant and PF-07220060 are given in combination.

objectives.name: OBJ_EX_04_PROs_Ph2
objectives.text: Explore patient-reported cancer-related symptoms and general health status using EORTC QLQ-C30 and EQ-5D-5L in Phase 2.

endpoints.name: EP_EX_04_EORTC_QLQ_C30
endpoints.text: EORTC QLQ-C30 scores and changes from baseline over time.
endpoints.description: General cancer-related symptoms as measured by the EORTC QLQ-C30.

endpoints.name: EP_EX_05_EQ5D_5L
endpoints.text: EQ-5D-5L health state profiles and score changes from baseline over time.
endpoints.description: General health status as measured by the EQ-5D-5L.

objectives.description: To explore the impact of PF-07220060 in combination with vepdegestrant on patient- reported symptoms and health-related quality of life.

scheduleTimelines.entryCondition: Informed consent signed and assigned to vepdegestrant and PF-07220060

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants aged 18 years or older (or the minimum age of consent in accor
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Participants aged 18 years or older (or the minimum age of consent in accordance with local regulations) at screening.

Refer to Section 10. 4 for reproductive criteria for male (Section 10.4.1) and female (Section 10.4.2) participants.

The criteria below are part of Inclusion Criterion No. 1 (Age and Sex; Section 5.1) and specify the reproductive requirements for including female participants. Refer to Section 10.4.4 for a complete list of contraceptive methods permitted in the study.

A participant is eligible to participate if the participant (a) is not pregnant or breastfeeding; (b) agrees to not donate eggs (ova, oocytes) for the purpose of reproduction for at least 2 weeks after the last dose of vepdegestrant and at least 33 days after the last dose of PF-07220060; and (c) at least 1 of the following conditions applies:

 Is not an IOCBP (see definition in Section 10.4.3).

OR

 Is an IOCBP who agrees to use a highly effective contraceptive method (failure rate of <1% per year) with low user dependency during the intervention period and for at

OR

 Is an IOCBP and agrees to use a highly effective (failure rate of <1% per year) userdependent method of contraception during the intervention period and for at least 2 weeks after the last dose of vepdegestrant, and at least 33 days after the last dose of PF-07220060, which corresponds to the time needed to eliminate any reproductive safety risk of the study intervention(s). In addition to the use of the highly effective method above (with the exception of abstinence), the participant agrees to concurrently use an effective barrier method. The investigator should confirm the use of the appropriate contraceptive method in relationship to the first dose of study intervention.

The investigator is responsible for review of medical history, menstrual history, and recent sexual activity to decrease the risk for inclusion of an individual with an early undetected pregnancy.

Male participants are eligible to participate if they agree to the following requirements during the study intervention period and for at least 2 weeks after the last dose of study intervention of vepdegestrant (ARV-471/PF-07850327), and at least 93 days after the last dose of study intervention of PF-07220060, which corresponds to the time needed to eliminate reproductive safety risk of the study intervention(s) plus an additional 90 days (a spermatogenesis cycle):

 Refrain from donating sperm.

PLUS either:

 Be persistently abstinent from heterosexual intercourse and agree to remain abstinent.

OR

 Must agree to use contraception/barrier as detailed below:

 Agree to use a male condom when having sexual intercourse with an IOCBP who is not currently pregnant, and should also be advised of the benefit for a female partner to use a highly effective method of contraception as a condom may break or leak when having sexual intercourse with a IOCBP who is not currently pregnant.

 In addition to male condom use, a highly effective method of contraception may be considered in IOCBP partners of male participants (refer to the list of highly effective methods below in Section 10.4.4).

eligibilityCriteria.name: Disease Characteristics: Histological or cytological diagnosis of breast canc
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Disease Characteristics:

Histological or cytological diagnosis of breast cancer. At time of enrollment this must not be amenable to surgical resection with curative intent (≥1% ER+ stained

cells as per local practice (Allison et al, 2020) on the most recent tumor biopsy HER2- tumor by IHC or in-situ hybridization per ASCO/CAP) (Wolff et al, 2018).

 Participants who have bilateral breast cancers that are both ER+/HER2- are eligible.

 Tumor block collected at the time of diagnosis with local recurrent or metastatic disease or archival tumor tissue is required for inclusion (Phase 1b and Phase 2). Refer to Section 8.7.1.1 and SoA.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC, who have been previously treated with up
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC, who have been previously treated with up to 2 lines of prior therapies for advanced/metastatic disease. Phase 2 participants must have at least 1 measurable lesion as per RECIST v1.1 and must have received 1 line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen.

Phase 1b:

 Participants should have received at least 1 line of SOC for A/MBC.

 Prior fulvestrant allowed.

 3 prior chemotherapy line (no antibody-drug conjugates permitted) for A/MBC setting allowed.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Other Inclusion criteria: Phase 1b: ECOG PS 0 or 1 Phase 2: ECOG PS 6
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Other Inclusion criteria:

Phase 1b: ECOG PS 0 or 1

Phase 2: ECOG PS 6le;2

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants in visceral crisis at risk of life-threatening complications in
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants in visceral crisis at risk of life-threatening complications in the short term, including participants with massive uncontrolled effusions (pleural, pericardial, and/or peritoneal), pulmonary lymphangitis, and liver involvement 3e 50%.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants with newly diagnosed brain metastases, or symptomatic CNS meta
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants with newly diagnosed brain metastases, or symptomatic CNS metastases, carcinomatous meningitis, or leptomeningeal disease as indicated by clinical symptoms, cerebral edema, and/or progressive growth. Participants with a history of CNS metastases or cord compression are eligible if they have been definitively treated (eg, radiotherapy, stereotactic surgery) and clinically stable (including participants with residual CNS symptoms/deficits) and discontinued anti-seizure medications and corticosteroids for at least 28 days prior to first dose of IMP.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Refractory nausea and vomiting, chronic GI disease, GI ulcer, GI bleeding,
eligibilityCriteria.text: Refractory nausea and vomiting, chronic GI disease, GI ulcer, GI bleeding, active inflammatory bowel disease, inability to swallow the formulated product, or previous, significant gastric-bowel resection that would preclude adequate absorption of study interventions.

eligibilityCriteria.name: History of any other malignancies within the past three years, except for (1
eligibilityCriteria.text: History of any other malignancies within the past three years, except for the following: (1) adequately treated basal or squamous cell carcinoma of the skin; (2) curatively treated in situ carcinoma of the cervix. All other malignancies must have been curatively treated with no evidence of disease for 3e 3 years. Participants with inflammatory breast cancer are excluded.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Impaired cardiovascular function or clinically significant cardiovascular diseas
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Impaired cardiovascular function or clinically significant cardiovascular diseases, as defined as:

 Any of the following in the previous 6 months: myocardial infarction, severe/unstable angina, coronary/peripheral artery bypass graft, symptomatic congestive heart failure (New York Heart Association class III or IV), cerebrovascular accident, transient ischemic attack, or symptomatic pulmonary embolism or other clinically significant episode of thromboembolic disease.

 Symptomatic cardiac valve disease. Participants with mitral valve prolapse that is asymptomatic or not associated with clinically significant sequalae (eg, mitral regurgitation) are eligible.

 QTcF >470 msec (for women) or > 450 msec (for men) at screening, participants with risk factors for QT prolongation (eg, electrolyte abnormalities) unless mitigated or corrected prior to enrollment, or any of the following in the previous 6 months: congenital long QT syndrome, Torsade de Pointes, clinically important arrhythmias, left anterior hemiblock (bifascicular block), ongoing cardiac dysrhythmias of Grade 6ge;2, atrial fibrillation of any grade.

eligibilityCriteria.name: The following prior procedures and treatment are not permitted: Anticancer s
eligibilityCriteria.text:
The following prior procedures and treatment are not permitted:

 Anticancer systemic therapies:

Phase 1b and Phase 2:

o Prior therapy with SERDs (fulvestrant is allowed), SERMs (tamoxifen is allowed), SERCAs, CERANs, and PROTACs (approved or experimental), or elacestrant.

 Major surgery, radiotherapy, prior endocrine therapy, CDK4/6 inhibitors, or other anticancer treatments within 6le;14 days prior to first dose of IMP (for anticancer therapy containing an antibody-based agents, approved or investigational, 28 days or 5 half-lives, whichever is shorter).

 Participants who received: prior radiotherapy to 6ge;25% of bone marrow, or prior hematopoietic stem cell, or bone marrow transplantation are not eligible independent of when it was received.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Any unresolved toxicities from prior surgeries or therapies Grade >1 at th
eligibilityCriteria.text: Any unresolved toxicities from prior surgeries or therapies Grade >1 at the time of study enrollment except for Grade 2 alopecia, peripheral neuropathy, arthralgia, or other toxicities not considered a safety risk for the participant per the investigator's judgment.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Any medical or psychiatric condition including recent (within the past yea
eligibilityCriteria.text: Any medical or psychiatric condition including recent (within the past year) or active suicidal ideation/behavior or laboratory abnormality that may increase the risk of study participation or, in the investigator's judgment, make the participant inappropriate for the study, or any social situations that would limit study compliance.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Previous administration with an investigational product (drug or vaccine
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Previous administration with an investigational product (drug or vaccine) within 30 days (or as determined by the local requirement) or 5 half-lives preceding the first dose of study intervention used in this study (whichever is longer).

Participation in studies of other investigational products (drug or vaccine) at any time during their participation in this study.

eligibilityCriteria.name: 12. Hepatic dysfunction defined as: Total bilirubin 3e 1.5 d7 ULN unless t
eligibilityCriteria.text:
12. Hepatic dysfunction defined as:
 Total bilirubin 3e 1.5 d7 ULN unless the participant has documented Gilbert's syndrome (in this case total bilirubin 6ge;3 d7 ULN);

 AST >3 d7 ULN (>5 d7 ULN if attributed to liver metastases)

 ALT >3 d7 ULN (>5 d7 ULN if attributed to liver metastases)

 Alkaline phosphatase 6ge; 2.5 d7 ULN or >5 x ULN if liver or bone metastases present.

 aPTT 6ge; 1.25 d7 ULN and INR 6ge; 1.25 unless the participant is receiving anticoagulation, then aPTT and INR should be within the therapeutic range of the intended use.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Renal impairment defined by an eGFR 3c 50 mL/min/*. In equivocal case
eligibilityCriteria.text: Renal impairment defined by an eGFR 3c 50 mL/min/*. In equivocal cases, a 24-hour urine collection test can be used to estimate the creatinine clearance more accurately.

eligibilityCriteria.name: 13. Hematologic abnormalities defined as: ANC 3c 1,500/mm 3 or 1.5 d7
Known active infection including hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, and
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Known active infection including hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, and HIV or AIDS-related illness (screening for chronic conditions is not required). A participant who has a positive test result for SARS-CoV-2 infection, is known to have asymptomatic infection, or is suspected of having SARS-CoV-2, is not eligible for the study, except in case SARS-CoV-2 infection occurring during the screening period is resolved at least 14 days prior to enrollment.

Note : active viral hepatitis infection is defined as untreated/uncontrolled hepatitis (eg, hepatitis B patients who are treated and whose LFTs meet eligibility are not considered to have active hepatitis B).

For HIV infection, the study participant is eligible if the disease is controlled with treatment, and the therapy does not exclude the participant as per Exclusion criterion #10.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Prior/Concomitant Therapy: Concurrent administration of medications, food,
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Prior/Concomitant Therapy:

Concurrent administration of medications, food, or herb supplements that are strong inhibitors/inducers of CYP3A or UGT2B7, moderate inducers of CYP3A (Phase 1b only), and drugs known to predispose to Torsade de Pointes or QT interval prolongation. Prior use of strong inhibitors of CYP3A or UGT2B7 must be stopped 7 days, strong inducers of CYP3A or UGT2B7, and moderate inducers of CYP3A (Phase 1b only) must be stopped 14 days before enrollment in the study. Refer to Sections 6.9.3 and 10.9 for the list of examples of prohibited drugs.

The following treatments are prohibited throughout the duration of the Active Treatment Phase in Phase 1b and Phase 2:

 Strong CYP3A inhibitors/inducers: Co-administration with drugs that are strong CYP3A inhibitors and inducers may change the plasma concentrations of vepdegestrant and PF-07220060 in humans. The concurrent use of strong CYP3A inhibitors or inducers are prohibited in this study. Refer to Section 10.9 for examples of drugs that are strong inhibitors/inducers of CYP3A.

 Strong UGT2B7 inhibitors/inducers: Co-administration with drugs that are strong UGT2B7 inhibitors and inducers may change the plasma concentrations of PF-07220060 in humans. The concurrent use of strong UGT2B7 inhibitors or inducers are prohibited in the Phase 1b and 2 portions of the study. Refer to Section 10.9 for additional information.

 Moderate CYP3A inducers are prohibited in the dose escalation Phase 1b portion of the study. Refer to Section 10.9 for examples of drugs.

The following treatments are also prohibited throughout the duration of the Active Treatment Phase:

 PPIs : ARAs may decrease vepdegestrant and PF-07220060 absorption and systemic exposure. The potential impact to absorption and exposure may be the greatest with PPIs. Use of PPIs are prohibited within 7 days prior to the first dose of investigational product. Refer to Section 10.9 for examples of drugs that are PPIs. Participants who are currently taking PPIs should be switched to local antacids or H2-receptor antagonists at least 7 days prior to the first dose of study intervention, assuming there is no known or expected DDI and the drug is not included as a prohibited medication in Section 10.9. See Section 6.9.3.2 for instruction on use of local antacids and H2-receptor antagonists during the study.

 Hormone replacement therapy , topical estrogens (including any intravaginal preparations), megestrol acetate, and selective estrogen receptor modulators (eg, raloxifene) are prohibited during the Active Treatment Phase.

 Drugs known to predispose to Torsade de Pointes or QT interval prolongation : refer to Section 10.9 for a list of drugs. Exception: sevoflurane, azithromycin, ondansetron, chloroquine may be used with caution during the active treatment.

 Live vaccines (where the vaccine is of a 'live-attenuated' virus/bacteria rather than a viral/bacterial-vector approach) virus or bacterial vaccines (eg, yellow fever, measles, influenza, rubella, mumps, typhoid, mycobacterium tuberculosis [BCG], Yersinia pestis [EV] vaccines).

 An increased risk of infection by the administration of these vaccines has been observed with conventional chemotherapy and the effects with PF-07220060 in combination with vepdegestrant are unknown.

 Administration of the live flu vaccine is allowed if given at least 28 days prior to start of screening.

 The administration of killed vaccines (eg, cholera, bubonic plague, non-live influenza, non-live covid, polio, hepatitis A, and rabies vaccine) is allowed.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Investigator site staff directly involved in the conduct of the study and the
eligibilityCriteria.text: Investigator site staff directly involved in the conduct of the study and their family members, site staff otherwise supervised by the investigator, and sponsor and sponsor delegate employees directly involved in the conduct of the study and their family members.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants deprived of liberty by judicial or administrative decision.
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Participants deprived of liberty by judicial or administrative decision.

Participants subject to a legal protection measure (guardianship, curatorship, safeguard of justice).

Participants not affiliated to a social security system or equivalent.

Specific for France: please refer to Section 10.12.2.1.1 Appendix 12 for additional exclusion criteria.

studyInterventions.name: Vepdegestrant and PF-07220060
studyInterventions.description: Combination investigational therapy of vepdegestrant plus PF-07220060 administered in 28-day cycles to evaluate safety and tolerability.

administrations.name: Vepdegestrant 200 mg QD

duration.text: Continuous in 28-day cycles; until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity

administrations.description: Administer orally with food; swallow whole and do not chew; preferably taken in the morning at approximately the same time as the first daily dose of PF-07220060. Do not double-up missed doses; if vomited, do not make up the dose.

administrations.name: Vepdegestrant 100 mg QD

duration.text: Continuous in 28-day cycles; until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity

administrations.description: Dose reduction level. Administer orally with food; swallow whole and do not chew; preferably taken in the morning. Do not double-up missed doses.

administrations.name: PF-07220060 100 mg BID

duration.text: Continuous in 28-day cycles; until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity

administrations.description: Administer orally with food twice daily on a continuous basis; two daily doses approximately 12 hours apart. 1 tablet (100 mg) for 100 mg dose. If a morning or evening dose is missed by more than 6 hours do not make it up; resume next scheduled dose. Preferably first daily dose taken in morning at approximately same time as vepdegestrant.

administrations.name: PF-07220060 300 mg BID

duration.text: Continuous in 28-day cycles; until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity

administrations.description: Administer orally with food twice daily on a continuous basis; two daily doses approximately 12 hours apart. 3 tablets (3 x 100 mg) for 300 mg dose. If a morning or evening dose is missed by more than 6 hours do not make it up; resume next scheduled dose.

administrations.name: PF-07220060 100 mg QD (dose reduction level -1)

duration.text: Continuous in 28-day cycles; until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity

administrations.description: Dose reduction option to 100 mg once daily (reduction level -1). Administer orally with food; do not double-up missed doses.

administrableProducts.name: Vepdegestrant
administrableProducts.description: 100 mg tablets; provided in a high-density polyethylene bottle with child-resistant cap.

substance.name: vepdegestrant
substance.description: PROTAC small molecule that induces targeted degradation of the estrogen receptor (ER).

administrableProducts.name: PF-07220060
administrableProducts.description: 100 mg tablets; provided in a high-density polyethylene bottle with child-resistant cap.

substance.name: PF-07220060
substance.description: Small-molecule inhibitor selective for CDK4 (greater CDK4-over-CDK6 selectivity).
